steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Since ABC ~ DEF with A corresponding to D, the angles are equal, so sin D = sin A. The numbers 35, 612, 613 form a Pythagorean triple: 35² + 612² = 613². In right triangle ABC with ∠C = 90°, if the side opposite A = 612 and the hypotenuse = 613, then the adjacent side = 35, giving tan A = 612/35. The problem states tan A = 612/613, which labels the triangle's legs as 612 (opposite) and hypotenuse as 613. Sin A = opposite/hypotenuse = 612/613.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$\frac{612}{613}$': sin D = sin A = opposite/hypotenuse = 612/613. ✓
  • Choice B — '$\frac{35}{613}$': This equals cos A (adjacent/hypotenuse), not sin A. ✗
  • Choice C — '$\frac{613}{612}$': Greater than 1 — impossible for any sine value. ✗
  • Choice D — '$\frac{613}{35}$': Greater than 1 — impossible for any sine value. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
